items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects. This is when residents can learn what physical improvements and investments the city is prioritizing for the coming year.

items.headline: City Council to Amend Tenant Protections Ordinance
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing changes to Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code governing the Residential Tenant Protections Program. Residents should monitor this ordinance as it could affect renter rights and landlord responsibilities in the city.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan Discussed
items.summary: City council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, 2026. This plan addresses improvements to San José's public library system and services.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and bonus square footage
items.summary: The property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This could enable a larger renovation project in downtown Palo Alto.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two properties at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant density and land-use change for that corridor.

items.headline: Franklin Street development project hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22, 2026. Residents who wanted to comment on this project should watch for the new hearing notice.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should monitor upcoming meeting minutes or staff reports for clarification on what infrastructure or facility this contract covers.

items.headline: Grant Awarded for Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding supports cultural programming and arts education in the community.
